mnemonics.title: Constructor Rules
mnemonics.content: CONSTRUCTOR: C-opy O-nly N-o R-eturn T-ype S-ame N-ame O-verloadable P-ublic U-sually T-akes A-rguments R-uns A-uto

mnemonics.title: Order of Execution in Inheritance
mnemonics.content: BASE before DERIVED - Base class constructor runs before derived class constructor (like 'B' comes before 'D' in alphabet)

mnemonics.title: Copy Constructor Triggers
mnemonics.content: PASS RETURN INIT: P-ass by value, A-rgument, S-truct, S-et, R-eturn, E-xception handling, T-hrow, U-sequality, R-untime, N-ew, I-nitialize, T-ernary

mnemonics.title: Initializer List Purpose
mnemonics.content: C-O-N-S-T: C-onst members, O-bjects as members, N-on-static const, S-tatic members, T-emperamental (references) - must use initializer list

acronyms.usage: Used to initialize object from another object of same class
Base → Member objects → Derived class constructor
Efficient way to initialize data members in constructor

visualTips.visualization: Think of constructor as 'birth certificate' (initialization) and destructor as 'death certificate' (cleanup). Constructor name = ClassName, Destructor = ~ClassName
Imagine making a photocopy - copy constructor creates exact duplicate. Happens during: pass-by-value, return-by-value, initialization, and passing to constructor.
Think of ' : ' as an 'initializer bridge' connecting constructor to members - direct initialization vs assignment (default then assign = two steps)
Same door (constructor) but different keys (parameters) - you can enter (create object) using different keys

analogies.analogy: Like a default setting on a phone - no specific choices made, uses factory defaults
Like ordering a custom pizza - you specify toppings (parameters) to customize your object
Like using a rubber stamp - creates identical copy of existing impression (object)
Like a housekeeper cleaning up when you leave - automatic cleanup when object goes out of scope
